Container.Name: hermes
Container.Overview: A Telegram bot that lets your users report media issues to Seerr (the unified Overseerr/Jellyseerr successor), with optional self-service auto-fix that tells Radarr or Sonarr to delete the file and re-search.
Container.Changes: ### v0.1.0 - Initial release - Issue reporting via Telegram conversation flow - Optional auto-fix via Radarr / Sonarr with allowlist + daily rate limit
Container.Description:
A Telegram bot that lets your users report media issues to [Seerr](https://seerr.dev/), with optional self-service auto-fix.
**Features** - /issue conversation walks users through: search media, pick season/episode for TV, choose issue type (Video / Audio / Subtitles / Other), describe - Issues posted to Seerr with reporter identity preserved in the message body - Optional **auto-fix**: tells Radarr or Sonarr to delete the current file and trigger a new download - Auto-fix gated by an allowlist of Telegram user IDs (defaults to admin only) and a 3-per-user-per-day limit, with explicit confirmation - Works in DMs or in any Telegram group the bot is added to
**Before starting** 1. Create a bot via @BotFather on Telegram; save the token 2. Get your Telegram numeric user ID by DMing @userinfobot 3. Have your Seerr URL and API key ready 4. (Optional) Radarr/Sonarr URLs and API keys if you want auto-fix
**After starting** 1. In Telegram, send `/setprivacy` to @BotFather, select your bot, choose **Disable** (lets the bot see commands in group chats) 2. DM your bot `/start` for the welcome wizard and connection status 3. DM `/link <your-seerr-or-plex-username>` to link yourself 4. Tell your users to do the same
See the [project page](https://github.com/cajunflavoredbob/hermes) for full docs.

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: Stores user mappings and the auto-fix audit log.

Config.Name: Admin Telegram User ID
Config.Description: Your numeric Telegram user ID. Get it by DMing @userinfobot on Telegram.

Config.Name: Seerr URL
Config.Description: Base URL of your Seerr instance. Use IP, not localhost (the bot is in a container).

Config.Name: Seerr Public URL (optional)
Config.Description: Optional. Public/reverse-proxy URL of Seerr used only in user-facing links (e.g. https://seerr.example.com). Leave blank to use the Seerr URL above. API calls always use Seerr URL.

Config.Name: Radarr URL (optional)
Config.Description: Optional. Set together with the Radarr API Key to enable movie auto-fix. Example: http://192.168.1.10:7878

Config.Name: Radarr API Key (optional)
Config.Description: Optional. Radarr → Settings → General → API Key.

Config.Name: Sonarr URL (optional)
Config.Description: Optional. Set together with the Sonarr API Key to enable TV auto-fix. Example: http://192.168.1.10:8989

Config.Name: Sonarr API Key (optional)
Config.Description: Optional. Sonarr → Settings → General → API Key.

Config.Name: Auto-fix Allowlist (optional)
Config.Description: Comma-separated Telegram user IDs allowed to trigger auto-fix. Leave empty to allow only the admin.

Config.Name: Encryption Key (optional)
Config.Description: Fernet key used to encrypt stored Plex tokens at rest. If blank, Hermes generates one on first run and persists it to /data/encryption.key. Provide a key here only if you want to control it explicitly (e.g. to migrate from another install). Generate with: python -c "from cryptography.fernet import Fernet; print(Fernet.generate_key().decode())"
